notes: Standard structure for empirical papers in medicine, biology, and many applied sciences. Used as the default template when no venue is specified.

sections.name: abstract
sections.heading: Abstract
sections.notes: Background, objective, methods summary, key result, conclusion. Structured (Background / Methods / Results / Conclusion) in many journals.

sections.name: introduction
sections.heading: Introduction
sections.notes: Establish the problem (what is known), identify the gap (what is not known), state the objective and hypothesis. End with a one-sentence statement of what this paper does.

sections.name: methods
sections.heading: Methods
sections.notes: Reproducible detail: participants/materials, design, procedure, measures, analysis plan. Past tense. IRB/ethics statement if applicable.

sections.name: results
sections.heading: Results
sections.notes: Report findings without interpretation. Tables and figures as primary evidence. Past tense. Every claim must be supported by a number.

sections.name: discussion
sections.heading: Discussion
sections.notes: Interpret results in light of the hypothesis, compare with prior work, explain unexpected findings, state limitations, implications, future work.

sections.name: conclusion
sections.heading: Conclusion
sections.notes: One-paragraph takeaway. Some journals merge this into Discussion.

sections.name: references
sections.heading: References
sections.notes: Bibliography. Generated by manuscript-format from cite keys in source.md.
